zh.lifeSummary: 當MABEL ISABEL RANDOLPH於1256年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾時，她的父親Thomas Randolph11253歲，母親Isabel Martha Kilconquhar-2歲。她於大約1269年與SIR GILBERT HAMILTON在蘇格蘭雷福福爾布拉可哈爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1295年在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当MABEL ISABEL RANDOLPH于1256年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾时，她的父亲Thomas Randolph11253岁，母亲Isabel Martha Kilconquhar-2岁。她于大约1269年与SIR GILBERT HAMILTON在苏格兰雷福福爾布拉可哈爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1295年在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: RANDOLPH
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rand
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Rand, a pet form of Randolf. Compare Randolph , Randall , Randy , and see also Rance .
English: habitational name from Rand (Lincolnshire), Rand Grange (North Yorkshire), or Raunds (Northamptonshire), all of which derive from Old English rand ‘edge, border, rim’.
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic compound personal names formed with rand ‘rim (of a shield)’. Compare 1 above.

variants.name: Randerson
meanings: English (West and East Yorkshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Randolf (see Randolph ) + -son. In East Anglia the name may also have been influenced by a form of the now rare or extinct Rendlesham, itself from the place of that name in Suffolk. The placename probably derives from an Old English personal name Rendel (genitive Rendeles) + Old English hām ‘village, homestead’.

variants.name: Randolf
meanings: English and German: variant of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randall
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

nameMeanings.name: MABEL
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

nameMeanings.name: ISABEL
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When MABEL ISABEL RANDOLPH was born in 1256, in Renfrewshire, Scotland, her father, Thomas Randolph, was 11253 and her mother, Isabel Martha Kilconquhar, was -2. She married SIR GILBERT HAMILTON about 1269, in Blackhall, Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1295, in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, at the age of 39.

Elle a épousé SIR GILBERT HAMILTON environ 1269, à Blackhall, Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1295, à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 39 ans.
